title: Neocheiropteris zippelii
taxonomy: Neocheiropteris zippelii is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is classified under the family name Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns that are commonly found in tropical regions. The genus Neocheiropteris is a part of the family Thelypteridaceae and is native to tropical Asia. The species Neocheiropteris zippelii is further classified under the family name Thelypteridaceae, and the order Polypodiales. The classification of Neocheiropteris zippelii is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Thelypteridaceae, Genus: Neocheiropteris, Species: N. zippelii.

intro: Neocheiropteris zippelii, also known as Zippel's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neocheiropteris zippelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neocheiropteris zippelii is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of extracts and tinctures to treat various health conditions.
